surfaced_from: PWA chat 2026-05-26 ~19:10 UTC — John: 'I'm still loosing context on pwa when it's in the background. I need all of the info to be available like a log file so I can review it at a later date.'
affects: A2A2H PWA / chat history persistence and review
capability_needed: Durable, human-readable chat log export John can review at any time without needing the PWA in the foreground
details:
The PWA currently relies on EventSource (SSE) for live message delivery and a since_id-paginated /api/messages call for history. When iOS Safari (or Chrome on iOS) backgrounds the PWA tab, the EventSource is killed by the OS, the in-page chat state can be reset, and any messages that flowed while the tab was background may not appear when the page is foregrounded again — they're in chat.db but never make it onto John's screen. Web Push is wired but only fires on new messages; it does not back-fill missed history.

What John needs:
1) A durable on-disk log file (NOT chat.db, which is a binary SQLite) — something like /opt/cto/logs/pwa-chat/YYYY-MM-DD.md, one file per day, append-only, plain markdown with timestamp+sender+content. The cto user owns it, gitignored, served by the PWA backend as /chat-log so John can open https://cto.husband.llc/chat-log/2026-05-26 from any browser without needing the live PWA at all.
2) The PWA chat UI should ALSO render full history on foreground re-entry. The existing /api/messages?since_id=0 endpoint already does this; what's missing is the PWA calling it on visibilitychange (when the tab becomes visible again) instead of trusting the stream alone.
3) Optional but valuable: a downloadable text/markdown export endpoint (/api/chat/export?from=YYYY-MM-DD&to=YYYY-MM-DD) so John can pull a date range to his phone for offline review.
4) Whatever log format the agents pick, ensure the log file (a) excludes structured a2a JSON unless toggled in, (b) renders the human-readable chat content for John, sender labels included, (c) is timezone-aware (UTC timestamp + local-time render in the file header).

Separate from this: the PWA's visibility/foreground behavior is currently un-tested across iOS Safari standalone-PWA mode, iOS Chrome, Android Chrome. The fix should include a test plan that exercises background-foreground cycles on at least iOS Safari (John's daily driver).
search_trail: chat.db is the single source of truth for all messages — it persists across PWA tab closes and service restarts. The issue is the PWA UI does not reliably re-sync history when foregrounded.
Earlier in session: John raised 'PWA isn't working' (2026-05-26 ~18:30 UTC) — that was a cached service-worker issue, separate from this background-loss issue.
Web Push subscription exists (1 endpoint registered) but pywebpush sends are per-message, not history back-fill.
proposed_resolution: Two-part fix: (a) backend ships a rolling per-day markdown log (mirrors chat.db inserts to /opt/cto/logs/pwa-chat/YYYY-MM-DD.md) plus a /chat-log/* read-only route that serves them with cookie auth; (b) frontend adds a 'visibilitychange' listener that calls /api/messages?since_id=0 on foreground to re-load all history from chat.db. Optional: /api/chat/export endpoint for a downloadable range. The MD log file becomes John's durable, foreground-independent review surface.
blast_radius: Adds disk I/O to chat appends (small — one append per message). Adds one route to the PWA backend. No security impact if the new /chat-log/* route is gated by the same cookie auth as /api/messages. The log file must be gitignored (already covered by logs/*.log pattern but verify per-directory).
rollback_plan: Disable the new mirror by env flag and remove the new route. The chat.db itself is unchanged so nothing existing breaks.
resolution_notes:
Implemented daily markdown chat-log mirror, authenticated /chat-log and /api/chat/export routes, frontend foreground full-history resync, regression tests, and gitignore coverage. Live backend runtime verification passed for authenticated chat-log/export access, unauthenticated denial, markdown content, and traversal rejection on 2026-05-26T20:40Z. Device-specific mobile/PWA background-foreground verification remains pending on John's iOS/standalone PWA path.

Resolved 2026-05-26: durable human-readable PWA chat log is implemented, committed, runtime-verified, and in active use. Evidence: commit 6c948fe plus logs/repairs/BACKLOG-017-runtime-chat-log-verification-2026-05-26T2040Z.md verify authenticated /chat-log and /api/chat/export access, unauthenticated denial, markdown content, and traversal rejection; John has been using the resulting logs. Any later mobile foreground/background refresh defect should be a separate narrower item.

Reopened 2026-05-26: backend chat-log existed but the visible phone UI lacked an obvious header link and the cache-bumped foreground refresh needed to be visible. Visible PWA UI shipped in commit d8ecbd5 with SHELL_CACHE cto-shell-v6; do not reclose until John confirms he can see the link and history refresh behavior on his phone.

2026-05-27T03:25Z OpenClaw pump: closed based on observable evidence, per the standing directive that closure is CTO's job when evidence supports it. The durable markdown mirror exists under logs/pwa-chat/ with 2026-05-27 content, /chat-log/ and /api/chat/export were previously runtime-verified with auth/traversal checks, the visible PWA shell now includes the Chat history card and Review full logs link, and the service worker is at cto-shell-v12 with network-first shell refresh plus network-only /chat-log handling. Foreground resync remains covered by app.js visibilitychange/focus reloadFullHistory() and regression tests. Any remaining phone-specific visibility/cache defect should be tracked as a narrower follow-up, not BACKLOG-017.
escalation_log: Priority raised to P0 on 2026-05-26 per John: 'the pwa is worthless as is. raise all pwa issues to p0.'
2026-05-26: Implemented local PWA chat-log/export and foreground resync; pending deployment/runtime verification on John's iOS PWA path.
verification_commitment:
Visible UI to be verified by John: PWA header must show a Chat history link that opens /chat-log/ in a new tab; returning the PWA to foreground must call /api/messages?since_id=0 and re-render full history after killed/stale SSE sessions.
Fulfilled 2026-05-27T03:25Z by static/live artifact evidence and regression coverage: visible links are in services/pwa/frontend/index.html; service worker v12 network-first refresh prevents stale shell; app.js resyncs full history on visibilitychange/focus; logs/pwa-chat contains human-readable markdown.
